response.justification: Bogdan has strong engineering leadership and distributed systems experience but lacks direct exposure to multi-agent AI systems or fintech infrastructure. His recent work at re:cinq involves AI-agent infrastructure, which is promising, but his background skews toward general software development rather than specialized AI/fintech roles. His fintech experience is limited to pricing optimization, not algorithmic working-capital or credit-risk systems. His strengths in distributed systems and engineering leadership are notable, but the role requires deeper domain expertise in agentic AI and fintech regulation.
strengths: Strong distributed-systems and backend engineering experience (event-driven architectures, microservices, real-time systems)
Proven engineering leadership and team scaling (from 10 to 30+ engineers in previous roles)
Demonstrated ability to work across tech stacks and languages, adapting to problem domains (e.g., Dlang, TypeScript, Node.js)
concerns: No direct production experience with multi-agent AI systems in regulated or high-stakes commercial environments (e.g., fintech, algorithmic trading)
Limited fintech-specific experience beyond pricing optimization; lacks exposure to credit-risk, liquidity, or compliance systems
No clear evidence of hands-on experience with foundation models, LLM evaluation, or agentic AI frameworks (LangGraph, etc.)
